recipes.title: Doro Wat
recipes.description: Iconic Ethiopian chicken stew simmered in a deeply spiced berbere and onion sauce, served with hard-boiled eggs.
steps: Marinate the chicken legs in lemon juice and salt for 30 minutes, then pat dry.
In a heavy pot, dry-cook the chopped onions over low heat for 25 minutes until deeply softened, stirring often.
Add the niter kibbeh, garlic, and ginger. Cook for another 5 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the berbere and tomato paste. Toast the spice paste for 3-4 minutes to bloom the flavors.
Add 250 ml water to loosen the sauce, then nestle in the chicken legs and coat them well.
Cover and simmer over low heat for 45 minutes, turning the chicken occasionally.
Score the hard-boiled eggs and add them to the pot for the last 10 minutes.
Taste and adjust salt. Serve hot with injera.

recipes.title: Injera
recipes.description: Spongy sourdough flatbread made from teff flour, the foundational staple of Ethiopian and Eritrean cuisine.
steps: In a large bowl, mix the teff flour with water until smooth and lump-free.
Cover loosely and leave at room temperature for 2-3 days to ferment until bubbly and tangy.
Stir in the salt and adjust consistency with water until it resembles a thin pancake batter.
Heat a wide non-stick pan over medium-high heat with no oil.
Pour batter in a spiral from outside to center, covering the pan in one thin layer.
Cover with a lid and steam for 2-3 minutes until the surface is fully covered with small holes (eyes) and edges lift.
Remove without flipping and cool on a clean cloth. Stack as you go.
Serve fresh with stews ladled directly on top.

recipes.title: Tibs
recipes.description: Quick-fired Ethiopian sauté of beef or lamb with onions, peppers, and rosemary in spiced butter.
steps: Heat the niter kibbeh in a heavy skillet until shimmering hot.
Add the beef cubes and sear over high heat for 4-5 minutes until browned on all sides.
Add the garlic, rosemary, and chilies. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
Toss in the onion and tomato, cook for another 3-4 minutes until just softened but still crisp.
Sprinkle the berbere, salt, and pepper over the meat and toss to coat.
Cook for 2 more minutes to bring everything together.
Serve sizzling on injera or with rice.

recipes.title: Kitfo
recipes.description: Ethiopian dish of finely minced raw beef seasoned with mitmita and warmed niter kibbeh.
steps: Trim the beef of any sinew and chill until very cold.
Mince the beef extremely finely by hand with a sharp knife until almost a paste.
Warm the niter kibbeh gently until just liquid, not hot.
In a bowl, combine the minced beef with mitmita, korarima, salt, and warmed niter kibbeh.
Mix vigorously with a wooden spoon until silky and uniform.
Plate immediately, accompanied by ayib cheese and gomen on the side.
Serve with injera and eat fresh; it must not sit out.

recipes.title: Shiro
recipes.description: Smooth, savory chickpea flour stew spiced with berbere, a daily staple across Ethiopia and Eritrea.
steps: Cook the onions in a dry pot over medium heat until softened, about 8 minutes.
Add the niter kibbeh and garlic, cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the berbere and tomato, toasting for 3 minutes.
Pour in the water and bring to a gentle boil.
Slowly whisk in the shiro flour to avoid lumps.
Reduce heat and simmer, stirring frequently, for 15-20 minutes until creamy and thick.
Adjust salt and consistency with hot water if needed.
Serve in a clay dish on injera, drizzled with extra niter kibbeh.

recipes.title: Misir Wat
recipes.description: Ethiopian red lentil stew built on caramelized onions and berbere, eaten on fasting days.
steps: Rinse the lentils thoroughly until the water runs clear.
Dry-cook the onions in a heavy pot for 15 minutes until deep golden, stirring often.
Add the oil, garlic, and ginger, cook for 3 minutes.
Stir in the berbere and tomato paste, toasting for 4 minutes until oil separates.
Add the lentils and water, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 30-35 minutes until lentils break down.
Stir occasionally and add water if it dries out. Season with salt.
Serve hot scooped onto injera.

recipes.title: Beyaynetu
recipes.description: Ethiopian fasting platter of assorted vegetable and legume stews arranged on injera.
steps: Prepare each component stew separately in advance and keep warm.
Lay a large injera flat on a round serving platter.
Spoon misir wat onto one section of the injera.
Add shiro, kik alicha, atakilt, gomen, fosolia, and salata in distinct mounds around the platter.
Place rolled extra injera pieces on the edge of the platter.
Garnish with a slice of fresh chili if desired.
Serve communally; diners tear off injera to scoop each stew.

recipes.title: Kik Alicha
recipes.description: Mild Ethiopian yellow split pea stew gently turmeric-tinted, free of berbere heat.
steps: Soak the yellow split peas in cold water for 1 hour, then drain.
Cook the onions in oil over medium heat for 8 minutes until soft.
Add the garlic, ginger, and turmeric. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add the split peas and water, bring to a boil.
Lower the heat, add the green chili, cover, and simmer for 40 minutes.
Stir occasionally and mash some peas against the side for body.
Season with salt and cook 5 more minutes uncovered.
Serve warm on injera as part of a beyaynetu platter.

recipes.title: Gomen
recipes.description: Ethiopian sautéed collard greens with garlic, ginger, and a hint of green chili.
steps: Wash the collard greens, remove tough stems, and chop finely.
Boil the greens in salted water for 8 minutes, then drain and squeeze.
Heat oil in a pan and sauté onions for 6 minutes until translucent.
Add garlic, ginger, and chili. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add the drained greens and stir to coat.
Splash in water and cook covered for 10 minutes over low heat.
Season with salt and cook uncovered another 3 minutes.
Serve as a side on a beyaynetu platter.

recipes.title: Atakilt Wat
recipes.description: Ethiopian mild stew of cabbage, carrots, and potatoes lightly turmeric-spiced.
steps: Heat oil in a wide pot and sauté the onion for 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger, and turmeric. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add the carrots and potatoes, stir to coat.
Cover and cook over medium-low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the cabbage and salt, fold in gently.
Cover and steam for 15 more minutes until vegetables are tender.
Uncover and cook 3 minutes to evaporate excess moisture.
Serve warm with injera.

recipes.title: Dabo Kolo
recipes.description: Crunchy Ethiopian roasted barley or wheat snack flavored with berbere or sweet spices.
steps: In a bowl, combine flour, berbere, sugar, and salt.
Add the oil and rub it into the flour mixture.
Slowly add water and knead into a stiff dough.
Rest the dough covered for 15 minutes.
Roll into thin ropes and cut into small pea-sized pieces.
Bake in a 175°C oven on lined trays for 30-35 minutes, stirring twice, until golden and crisp.
Cool completely before storing in an airtight jar.
Serve as a snack with coffee.

recipes.title: Doro Tibs
recipes.description: Pan-seared chicken pieces with onions, jalapeños, and rosemary in spiced butter.
steps: Marinate the chicken in lemon juice and salt for 20 minutes.
Heat niter kibbeh in a skillet over high heat.
Add chicken and sear for 6 minutes until browned.
Add garlic, rosemary, and jalapeños. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add the onion and stir-fry for 3 minutes until just softened.
Sprinkle berbere over the chicken and toss to coat.
Cook another 3 minutes until chicken is fully done.
Serve immediately on injera.

recipes.title: Ful Medames
recipes.description: Ethiopian breakfast of mashed fava beans seasoned with onions, tomato, and chili, eaten with bread.
steps: Soak the fava beans overnight, then drain.
Boil the beans in fresh water for 90 minutes until very soft.
Drain and lightly mash, keeping some beans whole.
Heat oil in a pan and sauté garlic for 1 minute.
Add tomato and onion, cook for 4 minutes.
Stir in berbere, salt, and the mashed beans, adding a splash of cooking water.
Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring often.
Finish with lemon juice and chopped chili. Serve with bread.

recipes.title: Genfo
recipes.description: Thick Ethiopian barley porridge mounded with a niter kibbeh and berbere well in the center.
steps: Bring water and salt to a boil in a heavy pot.
Reduce the heat and slowly whisk in the barley flour.
Stir vigorously with a wooden spoon for 10-12 minutes until very thick and pulling from the sides.
Mound the porridge onto a serving plate, smoothing the top.
Make a deep well in the center.
Mix the niter kibbeh with berbere and pour into the well.
Spoon yogurt around the edges.
Serve hot, scooping from the porridge into the spiced butter well.

recipes.title: Yebeg Tibs
recipes.description: Ethiopian sautéed lamb with onions, peppers, and rosemary in spiced butter.
steps: Pat the lamb dry and season with salt and pepper.
Heat the niter kibbeh in a heavy skillet until very hot.
Add the lamb in a single layer and sear for 6-8 minutes until well browned.
Add the garlic and rosemary, cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in the onion, pepper, and tomato. Cook for 4 minutes.
Sprinkle the berbere and toss to coat.
Cook 3 more minutes until vegetables are crisp-tender and lamb is medium.
Serve sizzling on injera.

recipes.title: Sambusa
recipes.description: Ethiopian fried triangular pastry filled with spiced lentils or minced meat, served as a snack.
steps: Sauté onion in 2 tbsp oil until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, and berbere. Cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in the cooked lentils and salt. Mash slightly and cook 5 minutes until dry.
Cool the filling completely.
Cut wrappers into long strips. Fold a triangle pocket and fill with a tablespoon of mixture.
Continue folding into a tight triangle, sealing the end with flour paste.
Heat oil to 175°C and deep fry the sambusas in batches for 3 minutes until golden.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot with chili sauce.
